title: Net-New Story Gap Review — 2026-05-14 (HRREC-82977)
content:
Executive summary (for PM)

  • Two-way recruiting email still behaves like “send from Workday, read replies in an external mailbox” for most tenants—customer comms and training should say that explicitly.
  • Recruiting Communications purge scope is broad (messages, attachments, notification artefacts); pair every “keep history” UX promise with privacy admin verification steps.
  • Template override risk: tenant-level Recruiting Communications templates can supersede recruiter-selected bodies (blank-send precedent)—QA must stage deliberate override vs recruiter-template cases.
  • My Conversations header entry and SMS security patterns are a frequent mismatch vector; bell routing and new domains need a single cross-story decision.
  • Many child stories still have empty formal acceptance tables—treat backlog refinement as a release risk, not a polish pass.
  • XO adjacency is strong for Recruiting Email, conversation message status, and purge loops, but weak for bounce telemetry, generic REST send, and several agency-only enforcement questions—schedule engineering confirmations early.
  • Attachment and integration limits are described inconsistently across internal docs; standardise on recruiter-visible limits and server validation for this composer.
  • Reporting / activity stream / timeline parity (92035, 92013) needs an explicit inventory of CRFs and surfaces or regressions will be invisible until UAT.

Epic-level notes

  • Overlapping slices: compose headers (91987–91990), send/no-attach (91994), send/with-attach (92036), validations (91993), and errors (92003–92005) should share one REST + state-machine contract or QA will get conflicting mocks.
  • Unread/read markers (91997–91999) depend on a product decision still open in Jira text—downstream notification and badge stories (91975, 91946) should not close first.
  • Agency vs non-agency tabs, visibility, and compose (91991, 92007, 92008, 92029) share the same predicate problem—one architecture review reduces duplicate defects.
  • Lifecycle admin (92012) + closed-thread UX (92009, 92014) + purge (92011) must align on what “closed” means in data vs UI.
  • Placeholder stories (90852, 90853, 92022) should not absorb engineering capacity without acceptance boundaries.

This run: 46 Stories under HRREC-82977; per-story Salomon Jira details + internal KB + XO search (parallel bands); Deployment Agent thread for tenant + QA staging. Salomon/DA are only inside PM/QA cells.

Story Gap score (%) PM lens QA lens XO lens Severity / open questions

HRREC-90852
AG: 2-way Email Communication in Workday Recruiting

52% — thin Jira AC
  • Insufficient spec — human input required — keep bullets high-level until Jira AC is expanded.
  • No description or acceptance criteria in Jira for the AG.
  • From internal knowledge: Recruiting lacks in-product two-way email today; replies land in external inboxes. Other hits weak/UK payroll noise.
  • From typical tenant patterns: see Executive summary (Reply-To inbox, notifications setup, Recruiting Communications template override risk, 7MB attachment expectation, purge scope).
  • Insufficient spec — human input required — avoid inventing deep edge-case matrices until acceptance scenarios exist.
  • Exercise loading, empty, timeout, and two recruiters on one candidate.
  • QA Reply-To mailbox; bell vs header — My Conversations stays SMS-oriented.
  • Template corruption can break notifications (internal NPE-in-attachment-build pattern) — use a clean-template tenant.
  • Attachments: test just under/at/just over 7MB; purge Recruiting Communications after send.
  • Conversation / Conversational UI Conversation classes found; AG does not name instances or REST.
  • Risk from absence: Placeholder AG not tied to concrete XO/REST artefacts.

P2 — thin spec

Open questions:

  1. Confirm written acceptance ties to observable recruiter + candidate outcomes (not only UI chrome).
  2. Align attachment/size language with the 7MB outbound expectation for this channel and server-side validation parity.
